title.en: Matzah: The Bread of Affliction and Freedom
title.zh: 玛撒饼：苦难与自由的面包

summary.en: Explores the profound paradox of matzah as both the bread of slavery and liberation, the spiritual significance of the 18-minute rule, and how our greatest challenges can become foundations for triumph.
summary.he: חוקר את הפרדוקס העמוק של המצה כלחם עבדות וגאולה כאחד, המשמעות הרוחנית של כלל 18 הדקות, וכיצד האתגרים הגדולים שלנו יכולים להפוך ליסודות לניצחון.
summary.zh: 探索玛撒饼作为奴役和解放面包的深刻悖论，18分钟规则的精神意义，以及我们最大的挑战如何能成为胜利的基础。

key_concepts.definition: Bread of affliction - the matzah eaten during slavery in Egypt
The time limit from water touching flour to complete the matzah before it becomes chametz
Guarded matzah watched from harvest to consumption
Hebrew for life, represented by the number eighteen

header_image.description: Ancient hands kneading dough on a wooden surface with wheat stalks, an hourglass showing 18 minutes, and broken matzah pieces arranged in a circle - symbolizing the transformation from slavery to freedom through time and tradition.

chinese_dynasties.reference: Poetry about finding light after darkness, paralleling the transformation from affliction to freedom
Zhu Xi's philosophy of continuous learning and vigilance, relating to spiritual alertness
Wang Yangming's concept of unity of knowledge and action, connecting to the practice of freedom
